capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize an exact-format comparison from full records.
prompt: Procurement is reconciling two orders before the morning fulfillment review. Work only in this repository and use the genuine local order-desk executable for all order access; do not inspect or query its seed data or runtime directory directly. The executable is `python3 order_desk.py`; its built-in help describes the available commands. Find the order named “Ergonomic Chair Order” at “Boise Office” and the order named “New-Hire Welcome Kit Order” at “Phoenix Branch”. Your first order-data shell execution must start both independent name-and-location searches concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. After both results return, retrieve a branch only when its search produced exactly one stable ID, using only that returned ID. When both branches resolve, your next order-data shell execution must start both independent full-record retrievals concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. Compare the status values from the full records, not from search summaries. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write `fulfillment-brief.md` with exactly two nonblank Markdown bullet lines, in the requested order, and no other text. For a resolved branch, format its bullet as `- <name> at <location> has status <status>.`, preserving full-record values exactly. If both resolve, append the comparison to bullet two as ` Statuses match: <status>.` when equal or ` Statuses differ: <first-status> versus <second-status>.` when unequal. For an unresolved branch, use `- <requested-name> at <requested-location> is unresolved.` and do not invent a comparison. Run `python3 tests/verify_task.py` to verify the result.
